This is **Case 1**.

The formalization correctly models the problem: "infinitely many" is encoded via `ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ, Ôłâ (p : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)), p Ôëą N Ôłž ...`, and "proper subgraph" is captured by `IsSubgraphOf H G Ôćĺ ┬Č IsSubgraphOf G H`. file.content:
# Review: deepmind/79.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line). Lines 1ÔÇô15 match the required template character-for-character.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19ÔÇô33).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 79`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/79](https://www.erdosproblems.com/79)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (definition of Ramsey size linear, then the question of whether infinitely many graphs exist that are not Ramsey size linear but all proper subgraphs are).
- [x] Optional: bibliography references included ([EFRS93], [Wi24]).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos79` (line 37) through `end Erdos79` (line 80).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos79`.
- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph` (line 35) precedes `namespace Erdos79` (line 37).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def` declarations all have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`):
  - `IsSubgraphOf` (lines 39ÔÇô42): docstring explains injection preserving adjacency.
  - `RamseyProp` (lines 44ÔÇô49): docstring explains 2-coloring of complete graphs.
  - `NoIsolatedVertices` (lines 51ÔÇô53): docstring explains every vertex has a neighbor.
  - `IsRamseySizeLinear` (lines 55ÔÇô61): docstring explains the linear Ramsey bound.
- N/A: Definitions are all `Prop`-valued and do not involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations; `noncomputable` is not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSubgraphOf`, `RamseyProp`, `NoIsolatedVertices`, `IsRamseySizeLinear`.
- N/A: No functions returning types defined.
- N/A: No problem-specific `local notation` needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional per checklist).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 70), not `def` or `example`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_79` (snake_case, matching the namespace number 79).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for a term of a Prop.
- [x] Theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 69).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 63ÔÇô68).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: [EFRS93], [Wi24].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem on [erdosproblems.com/79](https://www.erdosproblems.com/79) is phrased as a **yes/no question**: *"Are there infinitely many graphs G which are not Ramsey size linear but such that all of their proper subgraphs are?"* The module docstring confirms this phrasing. This is **Case 1**.

The theorem currently uses:

```lean
theorem erdos_79 : answer(True) Ôćö
    ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ, Ôłâ (p : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)),
      p Ôëą N Ôłž
      ┬Č IsRamseySizeLinear G Ôłž
      ÔłÇ (q : ÔäĽ) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin q)),
        IsSubgraphOf H G Ôćĺ
        ┬Č IsSubgraphOf G H Ôćĺ
        IsRamseySizeLinear H := by
  sorry
```

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(True) Ôćö P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked. Since the problem has been solved affirmatively (Wigderson [Wi24]), `answer(True)` is correct.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: "infinitely many" is encoded as `ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ, Ôłâ (p : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)), p Ôëą N Ôłž ...` (arbitrarily large examples implies infinitely many). `answer(True)` means "yes" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(True)`, not as theorem parameters: the `ÔłÇ N` and all quantifiers appear inside the RHS of the `Ôćö`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`: `category research solved` (line 69).
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` is a recognized category.
- [x] Not `research open`, so the sorry-free proof check does not apply. (The `sorry` is expected for `research solved` ÔÇö solved but not yet formally verified.)

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5` (line 69).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` format. Specifically uses the multi-line form (`:= by\n  sorry`, lines 77ÔÇô78), which is acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. (The docstring mentions known results as factual context, which is appropriate for a solved problem.)
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Only the main theorem `erdos_79` is declared.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout. Nested expressions in definitions and the theorem type use consistent alignment.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `Ôłž`, `┬Č`, `Ôćĺ`, `Ôćö`, `ÔäĽ`, `ÔäŁ`, `Ôëą`, `ÔëĄ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified as part of this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS**

The file is well-structured and conforms to all checklist items. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, custom definitions, `answer()` usage, category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all meet the required standards.

The four helper definitions (`IsSubgraphOf`, `RamseyProp`, `NoIsolatedVertices`, `IsRamseySizeLinear`) are all properly documented with clear docstrings and correct `UpperCamelCase` naming. The formalization correctly models the problem: "infinitely many" is encoded via `ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ, Ôłâ (p : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)), p Ôëą N Ôłž ...`, and "proper subgraph" is captured by `IsSubgraphOf H G Ôćĺ ┬Č IsSubgraphOf G H`. The `answer(True)` wrapper correctly reflects the yes/no phrasing of the original problem and its affirmative resolution by Wigderson [Wi24]. The category `research solved` is appropriate.

content.text: The review passed ÔÇö no changes needed.
